text: We started the surgery with the Venu-Venus ECMO support with a forearm approach.

at the XI DaVinci platform, from anterior to back with the Cartier at the 6th intercostal space, the camera, the Marilyn and the tip-up fenestrate at the 7th intercostal space and the auxiliary part at the 10th intercostal space.
Besides the ECMO, the surgery was made with an on-site bronchoscopy showing the loss of continuity of the posterior tracheal wall until the beginning of the left main bronchus.
We started by dissecting the esgus vein.
And we decided to divide the vein to expose the posterior tracheal wall.
We used the bronchoscopy onset to help us to find the beginning and the end of the tracheal lesion.
After the vagus repair and the crucial bronchoscopy support, we were able to find the bikini of the lesion to restart the suture.
We performed the repair with a running suture with 3.0 Stratofix.
After the beginning of the suture, the posterior roll presented itself much better.
Care must be taken with adjacent structures, especially the esophagus.
The robotic platform allows us to employ the skill of a manual switcher
At this point, we realize that we will need a larger dissection of the subcarinoid space.
The end of the track of repair in the vicinity of the Caraina concerned us with the continue to the left main broncos.
So we choose to terminate the running suture and repair the left membrane broken lesion with interrupted suture with PDS 4.0.
No wear leak was seen in the saline test solution and we had a good load inflation at the end of the surgery.
This is the final appearance in the endoscopic view with a good tracheolumen without strictures.
